methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Pano AI is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Sonia Kastner and Arvind Satyam that has raised $89M to date. Valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Lionpoint Group, LNZA), with direct stage and sector alignment to KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy—all Series C frontier-energy plays.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's last known post-money valuation of $500M as a base, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $950M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector comparables (KoBold at $3.0B, Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B, and Amogy at $700M) produces a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $3.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $808M to $1.1B, centered at $975M. Stage-progression and peer-median methods converge closely, reducing model risk. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set ($3.0B versus $700M for comparable-stage companies) and sensitivity to recent capital deployment and burn trajectory relative to revenue inflection timelines. The median of $975M reflects a conservative stance within the peer range, appropriate to a Series B company absent disclosed unit economics.
